title: Encyclia hoehnei
taxonomy: Encyclia hoehnei is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Encyclia and is native to Brazil. Further classification places it within the subtribe Encycliinae, which is a part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The family to which it belongs is Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification reflects the plant's botanical relationships and its place within the orchid family tree.

intro: Encyclia hoehnei, also known as the 'Rat Tail Orchid', is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which provide numerous health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Encyclia hoehnei include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Encyclia hoehnei is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and as an anti-inflammatory agent.
